programmes.show_description: 3/3. In this final episode, Bronwen shines a spotlight on today's female country artists, from Lainey Wilson to Priscilla Block, Alana Springsteen, Julie Williams, Carly Pearce and beyond.

programmes.show_name: Best of C2C with Kiefer Sutherland
programmes.show_description: Join Kiefer Sutherland for a personal selection of songs from the Country 2 Country Festival archive - it's the very best of C2C in one handy package!

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Vernon unleashes a heavyweight selection of iconic 90s dance bass lines. Back-to-back bangers from Daft Punk, Roni Size, Double 99, Mr Oizo, Les Rythmes Digitales and many more!
Gary Davies, takes us into the 80s, when Belinda Carlisle, Joan Jett, Pat Benatar, Wendy James and Chrissie Hynde achieve massive success with their infectious pop rock sounds.
Jo Whiley wraps up the series in the 90s with Skin, Courtney Love, Shirley Manson, Alanis Morrisette and Dolores O'Riordan who captivated rock and indie audiences.
Talk Talk performing live at Hammersmith Odeon in 1986. Featuring Talk Talk, Life's What You Make It, Give It Up, It's My Life, I Don't Believe In You and Such A Shame.
Comedian and writer Bob Mortimer joins Vernon for an in-depth conversation about his life, choosing ten songs which have soundtracked that time so far.
The Reverend Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad spread hope and joy, with uplifting stories about life, love and modern spirituality, alongside a great soundtrack for Sunday morning.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Angela Griffin... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy's live from Salford celebrating Mother's Day and talking about gift etiquette. Plus, The Weekest Link quiz, Paddy's Timewarp and your Sunday Dinners.
Elaine's got a host of 2026 Olivier Award-nominated show tunes, plus your Break a Leg messages, the stage news, and country music-themed show tunes, because Radio Loves Country!
Bob Harris and Jackson Dean are backstage at C2C with guests Brooks and Dunn, Drake Milligan, Ashley Cooke and more, plus live main stage music from Belfast, Glasgow and London!
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1988.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1999.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers, just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
Paul guides you through 7 decades of music, with the stories behind classic tracks.
Grab some popcorn, we're off to the movies with Angela Griffin. Featuring songs by Gabrielle, Cynthia Erivo and Kate Bush.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: Songs from the big screen with Angela Griffin, featuring All Saints, Dusty Springfield and The Righteous Brothers.
Richie Anderson is live from Salford with your Monday Motivation to kick start a brand new week, plus Secret Star and This Week in Songs.

programmes.show_name: Alternative Sounds of the 90s with Dermot O'Leary
programmes.show_description: Dermot takes you back to 1990 with music from The Stone Roses, The Charlatans & St Etienne. Meanwhile, Dom Joly & Steve Cradock pick their favourite 90s bands.
Welcome! It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Airdrum Anthems.
Scott's got your morning sorted... with some very exciting tickets to give away! Plus, the Birthday Game and Easiest Quiz on the Radio.
Making your mid-mornings memorable, Vernon's bringing big tunes, big names and big laughs to weekdays. Whatever you're up to, Vern wants to hear from you - email vernon@bbc.co.uk.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
Sara gets you through teatime with giggles and some fab music. Plus she reveals the new phrase for this week's Totally Teavoted and a listener plays Groove Is in the Start.
Sara plays the biggest and best tunes to get you up dancing!
Edith Bowman brings her Shiny Happy Playlist, 30 minutes of feel-good music from 7-7.30pm to soundtrack listeners' weekday evening.
Edith Bowman sits in, handpicking her favourite new music from all genres of the musical spectrum and quality vintage songs too.

programmes.show_name: The Blues Show with Cerys Matthews
programmes.show_description: Guitarist and producer Robin Trower, former member of Procol Harum, joins Cerys to share his favourite blues records and chat about the upcoming 50th celebration of his Live! album.

programmes.show_name: The Good Groove with DJ Spoony
programmes.show_description: Feel-good tunes to make you groove. 'Dr. Beat' is also on hand to prescribe a perfect tune for a listener whose week hasn't got off to the best start!
